- After clicking (15,24): (12,37)=G, (15,24)=W, (43,50)=G
So clicking (43,50) toggled both (43,50) and (12,37).
And clicking (15,24) toggled both (15,24) and (43,50).
This means:
- (43,50) is linked to both (12,37) and (15,24)
- Clicking (43,50) toggles (43,50) and (12,37)
- Clicking (15,24) toggles (15,24) and (43,50)

- (12,37)=G (from step 69: "G@12,37")

- Step 70: Clicked (46,52) -> G@12,37 (no change)

Then at step 71, I clicked (12,37) which was G:
- After: (12,37)=W, (43,50)=G
So clicking (12,37) toggled (12,37) from G to W and (43,50) from W to G.
Then at step 72, I clicked (43,50) which was G:
- After: (12,37)=G, (43,50)=W

Then at step 73, I clicked (15,24) which was G:

So clicking (15,24) toggled (15,24) from G to W and (43,50) from W to G.
So the links are:
- (12,37) <-> (43,50): clicking either toggles both
- (15,24) <-> (43,50): clicking either toggles both
This means (43,50) is a hub that's linked to both (12,37) and (15,24). Clicking any of these three toggles (43,50) and one other.
Actually wait, let me re-check. When I clicked (12,37), both (12,37) and (43,50) toggled. When I clicked (43,50), both (43,50) and (12,37) toggled. When I clicked (15,24), both (15,24) and (43,50) toggled.
So the pattern is:
- Clicking (12,37) toggles (12,37) and (43,50)
- Clicking (43,50) toggles (43,50) and (12,37)
- Clicking (15,24) toggles (15,24) and (43,50)
This means (12,37) and (43,50) are paired, and (15,24) and (43,50) are also paired. But that would mean clicking (12,37) should also toggle (15,24), which it didn't.
Let me reconsider. Maybe the links are:
